entry.title: Washington Post: 4 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Ukrainian forces withdraw from Lysychansk; Kyiv says Donbas battle not over
  • At least 3 dead, several injured after shooting at Copenhagen mall
  • Judge asks U.S. if Saudi crown prince should be immune from suit
  • Russia claims key city in punishing conquest of eastern Ukraine
  • How green became the color of abortion rights
  • Wary of China threat, Taiwanese join Ukraine’s fight against Russia
  • Barriers to abortion in Canada make it an unlikely haven for Americans
  • A call to arms: Poland’s military and civilian defenses rethink the future
  • On the front lines, Ukrainian women are often the first responders
  • Fourth crew member rescued after engineering ship sinks
  • Bonnie grows into a hurricane in Pacific off southern Mexico
  • After 3 feet of rain, 32,000 in Sydney area may need to flee
  • 3 dead, 3 critically wounded in shooting at Denmark mall
  • Ricky Martin denies restraining order allegations
  • Latest migrant caravan breaks up in Mexico after 2 days
  • Alpine glacier chunk detaches, killing at least 6 hikers
  • Russia claims control of pivotal eastern Ukrainian province
  • Romanian woman killed in 2nd Egypt shark attack in days
  • Palestinian dies from shot by Israeli troops in West Bank
  • Akron police release video of officers shooting Black man dozens of times
  • These high school seniors raced away from graduation to fight a fire
  • The Rainbow Family comes to Colorado, bringing peace, love and anxiety
  • Multiple criminal referrals of Trump possible, Cheney says
  • Inflation is making homelessness worse
  • Bill to grant crypto firms access to Federal Reserve alarms experts
  • Texts, web searches about abortion have been used to prosecute women
  • OPEC+ Did Its Job, But Don’t Expect It to Disappear
  • Warren Buffett Has Advice for the Barbarians at Japan’s Gate
  • Is There Anything That Gen Z Won’t Drink?
  • The Beverly Hills of Singapore Shows Signs of Froth
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • Why Japan Will Struggle to Do Without Russian Energy
  • Nearly every American has a foreboding the country they love is losing its way
  • Don’t venerate or vilify the Founders. Vindicate their radical vision.
  • The court’s EPA ruling was about something much bigger than one agency
  • Why Glenn Youngkin — or someone like him — must run in 2024
  • This July Fourth, we’re torn over what we love about our country
  • This 1972 speech is more relevant than ever this Fourth of July
  • Democracy must not die in darkness
  • Distinguished person of the week: She showed her party what patriotism is
  • July Fourth is a time to sing our complicated country’s praises
  • Cartoon by David Horsey
  • Lithuania is enforcing E.U. sanctions against Russia
  • An advance for non-heterosexual medical students and health care
  • We can’t lose sight of the climate crisis
  • The mirage of equivalence
  • Solving the gun issue is harder than it seems
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 4 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 4 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-04 03:54AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Australia news LIVE: Albanese meets with Zelensky in Ukraine; Thousands of Sydney homes at risk of flooding as heavy rain continues in NSW
  • Quirky Melbourne busker is drawing crowds
  • NSW floods LIVE updates: Parts of Sydney told to evacuate as floodwaters rise across the city; BOM expects rain to ease later today
  • WorkSafe charges St Basil’s over COVID-19 outbreak that killed dozens
  • Moore good news for Pies as Darcy cleared; Bombers add AFLW mentor; Cats embrace ‘finals smell’
  • ‘Nick’s not the bully. I’m the bully’: The basketball crew that prepared Kyrgios for Wimbledon assault
  • ‘She’d left her husband’: Witness claims he saw Lynette Dawson alive
  • Suncorp shores up reinsurance as it prepares for NSW floods claims
  • Cargo ship in danger in wild weather off Sydney’s south
  • Best of cartoons, July 4, 2022
  • Risky strategy for picking winners and losers in the green economy

  • Virat Kohli blows a kiss to departing Bairstow
  • Adam Goodes shortlisted in new children’s literature award for First Nations stories
  • ‘New evidence’ to show Trump wanted to join Capitol riot
  • Government launches legal action to stop rail disruptions

  • Blues make shock selection for Origin decider, Maroons forced into one change
  • Cargo ship in trouble off NSW coast in wild weather
  • Driver smashes into police cars in Melbourne CBD
  • Australia is doing more to help Ukraine than some NATO members

  • Perth’s pub playground boom: social benefit or smart sales ploy?

  • What would a First Nations foreign policy look like?
  • Nicola Forrest challenges Anthony Albanese to take charge of early education overhaul
  • ASX pares back early gains as tech stocks lose momentum
  • ‘Baseless’: Australian biotech Cochlear steps up fight against US university over hearing device
  • Bauxite miners want to clear seven Rottnest Islands’ worth of WA forest

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 4 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • China downgrades Typhoon Chaba but heavy rain to fall in several provinces

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 4 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 4 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-04 03:46AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • How rail giant MTR Corp is moving Hong Kong closer to mainland China
  • ‘Hong Kong, add oil’ should not be taboo, says Canto-pop legend Jacky Cheung after mainland backlash over anniversary greeting
  • Alex Law, famed Hong Kong director and screenwriter behind Echoes of the Rainbow and An Autumn’s Tale, dies at age 69
  • ‘Epoch-making’ author Ni Kuang, who put Asian stamp on science fiction, dies in Hong Kong at age 87
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong lawmaker tests positive 2 days after posing for photo with Xi Jinping during 25th anniversary of handover celebrations
  • Plastic shopping bag charge should be increased to at least HK$2, says Hong Kong environmental group ahead of Legco meeting on levy
  • Chances of survival for 27 missing crew members of typhoon-hit vessel ‘very slim’, Hong Kong rescue authorities admit on day 2 of search
  • Hong Kong police lay holding charge of murder on man suspected of killing 23-year-old yoga instructor at 5-star hotel
  • Hong Kong leader John Lee convenes first cabinet meeting to discuss to-do list from Xi Jinping, vows to lobby for ‘limited measures’ to reopen border
  • Thousands show up for grand opening of Hong Kong Palace Museum to see 900 precious relics and national treasures on loan from Beijing
  • Hong Kong Sevens must be a proper comeback party, or we should wait
  • The Taiwanese musician mapping mainland China’s military sites – one airfield at a time
  • China still weighing up risks of engagement with Afghanistan’s Taliban government, experts say
  • Both G7 and BRICS lack the dominance to resurrect grand rivalries of Cold War power politics
  • Foreign Minister Wang Yi becomes most senior Chinese official to visit Myanmar since coup
  • Former Russian foreign minister warns ‘self-centred’ Nato risks deeper crisis over Ukraine
  • Chinese police officer’s false evidence claims raise fresh concerns about investigation into attack on female diners
  • Former Japanese prime minister urges Tokyo to talk more to China and stop trying to ‘encircle’ it alongside the US
  • Chinese food firm Maiquer under investigation over milk additive
  • After brutal attack, Chinese women are turning to self-defence classes
  • Climate change: melting Tibetan glaciers could unleash potentially dangerous bacteria, scientists say
  • Covid-19: new cases on the rise in China as travel rules ease
  • Tourists are returning to Japan’s Kyoto, but not everyone’s happy to see them
  • Why is Malaysia seeing a rise in data leaks, and what is the government doing to stop it?
  • Indonesian historian Onghokham’s ‘fearless’ life as gay Chinese man, Java expert celebrated in biography
  • Set in Bali, filmed in Australia: Ticket to Paradise starring George Clooney, Julia Roberts, Maxime Bouttier sparks debate on ‘colonial gaze’
  • UK PM under fire amid claims he knew about ‘sex pest’ lawmaker’s behaviour
  • Hundreds protest in US after release of video showing police killing black man
  • Thousands wounded and five dead in rare Uzbekistan protests: reports
  • Nigeria police rescue dozens, including children, held in church waiting for Christ’s second coming
  • 31 bodies, some decomposing, found at Indiana funeral home
  • Cologne’s Pride parade draws more than 1 million people in Germany
  • Shark kills two tourists from Austria and Romania in Egypt’s Red Sea
  • 3 dead and 3 in critical condition after shooting at Copenhagen shopping centre
  • Six hikers killed, 8 injured after Alpine glacier chunk breaks loose on Italian mountain
  • Greatest-ever number of Russian diplomats to leave Bulgaria, in mass expulsion
  • Man driving jet truck at more than 483kph dies in US air show crash
  • Russia claims control of last major Ukrainian city in eastern Luhansk
  • Legendary British theatre director Peter Brook dies aged 97
  • Swedish company develops plant-based ‘human meat burger’
  • How the US Supreme Court that Donald Trump filled is changing American life
  • HKEX, Sands China lead losses in Hong Kong as Covid-19 flare-up dents recovery bets while Ganfeng slides on insider trading probe
  • Why stock markets must stop acting like casinos, especially in Asia
  • New World scion’s family office plans equity hedge fund to capture improving market sentiment
  • Result-oriented John Lee must beware of procrastination traps
  • Hong Kong investors gain access to US$89.6 billion in China-based ETFs as expansion of Stock Connect scheme kicks in
  • Ganfeng, China’s largest lithium salts producer, is under probe for suspected insider trading
  • Typhoon dampens property investor spirits as few flats in new Hong Kong housing project find buyers
  • Emerging Asia at risk from EU’s protectionist carbon border tax
  • Hong Kong’s dry spell for new stock offerings set to end with big China IPO deals
  • Empowering teachers to build the classroom of the future
  • The Man Who Sold His Skin movie review: Tunisia’s Oscar nominee is a refugee drama doubling as a contemporary art satire
  • Forget being ‘Fujian people’ or Cantonese – we in Southeast Asian diaspora should see ourselves as citizens who happen to be of Chinese ancestry
  • Are common vitamins and supplements worth the money? They don’t do as much as we think, and some could be damaging, major study suggests
  • Chinese art museum’s European offshoot in Berlin closes, a victim of social and political turbulence and funding issues, its artistic director says
  • Blind Chinese make-up artist teaches thousands of visually impaired women to apply make-up using sense of touch
  • A Star Ferry sunset tour of Hong Kong’s Victoria Harbour aboard converted vessel the World Star – what’s it like?
  • Gender-fluid Miss Universe contestant on having a ‘gay aura’ and why Bhutan should be seen as an LGBTQ-friendly country
  • How Hong Kong craft brewery became a passion project for co-founder Scott Powrie, and his ambition to climb some of the world’s highest peaks
  • The warning behind Loewe’s eco-conceptual Paris Fashion Week show: Jonathan Anderson’s spring/summer menswear collection paired TV screen plates with clumps of grass for a dystopic vision
  • Meet the WWE’s new CEO, Stephanie McMahon: she’s disgraced Vince McMahon’ daughter, the wife of Triple H and also a former wrestler who is now in charge following her dad’s misconduct allegations
  • 14 celebrities who became first-time dads after 50, from Elton John and George Clooney, to Simon Cowell, Andy Cohen, David Letterman and Star Wars voice talent James Earl Jones
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 4 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • VOX POPULI: Workers in U.S. remind us of the importance of forming unions
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
